intro: At ESCO, I managed a $200M project, directly managed 8 engineers, and coordinated delivery with the vice president of ESCO and client representatives.

At Vendrix, I managed one engineer while leading the v2 frontend and backend release, ERP integrations, transaction-tagging design, and the GCP platform work behind a construction finance product. Foundation Software later acquired Vendrix.
After Vendrix, I applied the same operating discipline to EdgeWatch-style field systems and to consulting engagements in data platforms, forecasting, and grounded AI knowledge systems.

tracks.title: Management and delivery leadership
tracks.summary: Lead engineers, coordinate executives and clients, and keep major releases and programs moving without losing technical depth.
bullets: ESCO: managed a $200M project, directly managed 8 engineers, and coordinated with the vice president of ESCO and client representatives
Vendrix: managed 1 engineer while owning the v2 frontend and backend release across product work, integrations, and platform changes
Operate comfortably at both levels: stakeholder alignment, prioritization, and team delivery alongside hands-on architecture and implementation

tracks.title: Vendrix product and platform ownership
tracks.summary: Own the product and platform path together so releases, integrations, and operations mature as one system.
bullets: Led frontend and backend delivery for the v2 release while managing partner integrations and core platform work
Designed transaction and tagging schemas that let customers connect spend to budgets, projects, resources, tools, and equipment across ERP-linked workflows
Architected and operated production infrastructure on GCP with Cloud Run, Cloud Functions, IAM, Cloud Build, logging, monitoring, and migration work

tracks.title: EdgeWatch and field operations
tracks.summary: Build offline-capable operational systems where connectivity and device health are part of the architecture, not edge cases.
bullets: Offline-first PWA workflows for receipts, timesheets, photos, notes, and searchable exports
Buffered Raspberry Pi telemetry with heartbeats, threshold alerts, and device-health monitoring
Alert dedupe, throttling, and quiet-hours rules so operators see actionable events instead of noise

tracks.title: Data platforms and forecasting
tracks.summary: Design governed ingestion and analytics systems that turn manual feeds into decision-ready data products.
bullets: Event-driven ingestion with schema-drift handling, validation gates, and replay and backfill workflows
Curated warehouse modeling in raw-to-curated-to-marts layers with versioned published views and APIs
Geospatial analytics, alerting, and forecasting backtests tied to monitoring instead of spreadsheet-only workflows

tracks.title: Grounded AI knowledge systems
tracks.summary: Ship evidence-backed knowledge tools that improve lookup speed without giving up auditability, citations, or control.
bullets: Hybrid retrieval, citation-required responses, refusal mode, and regression harnesses for retrieval quality
Metadata strategy, document classification, audit logging, and restore drills for confidentiality-first environments
Operational AI that improves lookup speed and transition readiness without hiding its evidence trail

deliveryLoop.title: Harden the platform
deliveryLoop.description: Start with runtime choices, network boundaries, IAM, encryption, and failure modes that can survive real production traffic.

deliveryLoop.title: Automate the delivery path
deliveryLoop.description: Use CI/CD, migrations, and repeatable environment bring-up so the platform can keep moving without depending on manual heroics.

deliveryLoop.title: Instrument the system
deliveryLoop.description: Add logging, monitoring, alerts, and data-quality checks so problems surface early and troubleshooting stays bounded.

deliveryLoop.title: Make recovery explicit
deliveryLoop.description: Ship runbooks, replay and backfill procedures, restore drills, and operational checklists so incidents are survivable.
